findings.message: The brief assumes callers can emit guest_map_built / guest_map_build_failed with map size, duration, and error detail, but the current contract does not expose that information. buildGuestEmailMap() already catches Firebase HTTP/network failures internally and returns an empty Map, so sync.server.ts and recovery.server.ts cannot distinguish Firebase failed from there were simply no active guest emails. A viable plan must first change the guest-matcher return/error contract or emit telemetry inside guest-matcher.server.ts itself.
The must not add measurable latency constraint is under-argued. recordInboxEvent() is async and awaited, and adding one extra D1 write per thread would add real latency unless batched or deferred.
The batch-event thread ID guidance is too loose. Using the first thread ID would falsely attribute a batch-scoped issue to a real guest thread. Should narrow to synthetic batch-scoped ID only.
Recovery event-emission tests should be called out explicitly if that path ships in the same change.
